mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm client, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

```

> This command adds the specific vLLM repository for version 0.17.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace, enabling you to use its features.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
Creating a Custom Resource allows you to define the specific configuration for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This example CR configures vLLM to serve the 'gpt-3' model with 2 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> You should see the status of your vLLM instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the vLLM instance.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.17.0.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm list --namespace vllm' to check if vLLM is installed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs for vLLM may have already been installed.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd | grep vllm.io'. If they exist, skip the CRD installation step.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate issues with the model configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM CR.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory efficiency with PagedAttention.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm', showing the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:

kind: VLLM

  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
